# async-timer

> The missing Python async timer. Recurring async calls in asyncio with
> proper lifecycle, two delivery models (latest-value fanout and
> per-consumer buffered subscriptions), fixed-rate / fixed-delay
> scheduling, jitter, on-demand triggering, and cross-thread control.
> Zero runtime dependencies. Python 3.9+.

`async-timer` solves the problem behind code like:

```python
while True:
    await do_the_thing()
    await asyncio.sleep(5)
```

That naive loop drifts, has no graceful cancellation story, no way to
gate startup on the first successful tick, and no way for other tasks
to consume results. `async-timer` is what you actually want:

```python
import async_timer

async with async_timer.Timer(delay=5, target=refresh_db) as timer:
    await timer.wait(hit_count=1)   # block until first successful refresh
    # ...serve traffic; timer keeps refreshing in the background
```

## When to use this library

- Periodic refresh of in-memory caches in an asyncio app (FastAPI,
  Starlette, aiohttp, etc.)
- Metrics sampling on a stable wall-clock cadence
- Heartbeats (e.g. Redis liveness keys with TTL)
- Polling external state on an interval
- Any recurring async call that needs proper cancellation, startup
  gating, or fan-out to consumers

## When NOT to use this library

- Cron-style wall-clock scheduling ("every Monday at 09:00") — use
  APScheduler or aiocron
- Background *jobs* with retries, persistence, queues — use arq,
  dramatiq, or Celery
- Cross-process / cross-machine recurring tasks — use a scheduler +
  broker (Celery beat, arq cron)
- One-shot delayed call — use `asyncio.get_event_loop().call_later`

## Core API

- [`async_timer.Timer(delay, target)`](src/async_timer/timer.py) —
  the main class. `start()` / `join()` / `wait(hit_count=N)` /
  `cancel()`. Async context manager + async iterator.
- [`@async_timer.every(5)`](src/async_timer/decorators.py) —
  decorator that wraps a function into a Timer; original on `.func`.
- [`async_timer.TimerGroup()`](src/async_timer/group.py) — start,
  cancel, wait, and trigger a set of timers together.
- [`timer.subscribe(maxsize=0)`](src/async_timer/subscription.py) —
  per-consumer buffered feed; doesn't drop intermediate ticks.

## Key features

- **Two delivery models:** `join()` / `wait()` / `async for timer` is
  single-shot latest-value fanout. `subscribe()` gives each consumer a
  buffered queue that doesn't drop intermediate ticks.
- **Scheduling modes:** `fixed_delay` (next tick fires `delay` after
  the previous one *finishes*) or `fixed_rate` (anchored to wall
  clock; missed slots skipped + logged).
- **Cross-thread control:** `cancel_threadsafe()`, `trigger_threadsafe()`,
  `subscription.close_threadsafe()` for signal handlers, sync REST
  endpoints, and worker threads.
- **Restartable:** `start()` after `cancel()` works.
- **Test-friendly:** [`mock_async_timer.MockTimer`](src/mock_async_timer/timer.py)
  replaces real sleeps with an `AsyncMock`.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): full feature list, examples, thread-safety
  semantics, comparison with other libraries
- [Recipes](docs/recipes/): narrative walk-throughs explaining the *why*
  behind each pattern
  - [FastAPI lifespan: warm a cache before serving](docs/recipes/fastapi.md)
  - [Starlette lifespan](docs/recipes/starlette.md)
  - [aiohttp cleanup context](docs/recipes/aiohttp.md)
  - [Prometheus periodic metric sampling](docs/recipes/prometheus.md)
  - [Redis heartbeat with TTL](docs/recipes/redis_heartbeat.md)
  - [Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT](docs/recipes/graceful_shutdown.md)
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md)

## Runnable examples

Self-contained scripts under [`examples/`](examples/):

- [`fastapi_lifespan.py`](examples/fastapi_lifespan.py) — cache warmup
  before the app accepts requests
- [`fastapi_multi_cache_warmup.py`](examples/fastapi_multi_cache_warmup.py) —
  `TimerGroup.wait(hit_count=1)` to warm several caches with one await
- [`cache_invalidate_all.py`](examples/cache_invalidate_all.py) —
  `TimerGroup.trigger()` for an admin "refresh all caches" endpoint
- [`starlette_lifespan.py`](examples/starlette_lifespan.py)
- [`aiohttp_cleanup_ctx.py`](examples/aiohttp_cleanup_ctx.py)
- [`prometheus_metrics.py`](examples/prometheus_metrics.py) —
  fixed-rate sampling with `TimerGroup`
- [`redis_heartbeat.py`](examples/redis_heartbeat.py) — liveness key
  with TTL
- [`graceful_sigterm.py`](examples/graceful_sigterm.py) — clean
  shutdown from SIGTERM/SIGINT

## Project

- License: MIT
- Issues: https://github.com/IljaOrlovs/async-timer/issues
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/async-timer/
- Python: 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13
- Status: production-stable, 214 tests, 100% line coverage
